d4e1_influencer.speaker: Mentor
d4e1_influencer.text: Day 4! Your laundromat is getting noticed. A local social media influencer walks in and offers to promote your shop online in exchange for free laundry services.

d4e1_influencer_b.speaker: Influencer
d4e1_influencer_b.text: Hey! I have about 50,000 followers and I love this laundromat. I could feature it if you give me free washes. What do you say?

choices.text: Agree immediately — free exposure sounds great
Negotiate — offer a discount instead of free
Ask to see their engagement stats first
Decline politely — prefer organic growth

d4e1_agree.speaker: Influencer
d4e1_agree.text: Amazing! I'll post about this today. Your laundromat is going to blow up online, trust me!

d4e1_negotiate.speaker: Influencer
d4e1_negotiate.text: A discount instead of free? Fair enough. I respect the business mindset. Deal!

d4e1_verify.speaker: Influencer
d4e1_verify.text: Sure! Here are my analytics. Most followers are local and highly engaged. Pretty solid numbers, right?

d4e1_decline.speaker: Influencer
d4e1_decline.text: Oh, okay. No worries. Your loss, I guess. Good luck with the laundromat though!

d4e1_result_agree.speaker: Mentor
d4e1_result_agree.text: Influencer marketing can be powerful, but always set clear expectations upfront. A verbal agreement isn't enough.

d4e1_result_negotiate.speaker: Mentor
d4e1_result_negotiate.text: Smart negotiation! Never give away full value when a partial deal serves both sides. Business is about mutual benefit.

d4e1_result_verify.speaker: Mentor
d4e1_result_verify.text: Due diligence before any deal is essential. Follower count means nothing without real engagement from the right audience.

d4e1_result_decline.speaker: Mentor
d4e1_result_decline.text: Organic growth is valid, but don't dismiss partnerships outright. Evaluate each opportunity on its own merits.

d4e2_staff_discount.speaker: Mentor
d4e2_staff_discount.text: Midday. You discover one of your staff members has been giving unauthorized discounts to their friends. Revenue is slightly lower because of it.

d4e2_staff_b.speaker: You
d4e2_staff_b.text: This is a trust issue. How I handle this will set the tone for how my team operates...

choices.text: Have a private, calm conversation and set boundaries
Issue a formal written warning
Dismiss them immediately for breach of trust
Gather more evidence before acting

d4e2_talk.speaker: Staff
d4e2_talk.text: I'm really sorry. I didn't think it was a big deal, but I understand now. It won't happen again.

d4e2_warning.speaker: Staff
d4e2_warning.text: A written warning? I understand. I'll make sure this never happens again.

d4e2_dismiss.speaker: Other Staff
d4e2_dismiss.text: Wow, they got fired just like that? I had no idea it was such a serious offense. Everyone's a bit shaken.

d4e2_evidence.speaker: You
d4e2_evidence.text: I need to be sure before I act. Let me review the receipts and security footage carefully.

d4e2_result_talk.speaker: Mentor
d4e2_result_talk.text: Direct, honest communication resolves most workplace issues. People often make mistakes without realizing the full impact.

d4e2_result_warning.speaker: Mentor
d4e2_result_warning.text: Formal warnings create paper trails and signal seriousness. Good for repeat offenses but may feel heavy-handed for a first incident.

d4e2_result_dismiss.speaker: Mentor
d4e2_result_dismiss.text: Immediate dismissal sends a strong message but can damage team morale. Reserve it for severe or repeated violations.

d4e2_result_evidence.speaker: Mentor
d4e2_result_evidence.text: Never act on suspicion alone. Gathering evidence before confronting protects both you and your employee from unfair outcomes.

d4e3_corporate_buyer.speaker: Mentor
d4e3_corporate_buyer.text: Late afternoon. A corporate buyer wants to partner with your laundromat for bulk laundry services — a big contract, but they want a discount and faster turnaround.

d4e3_corporate_b.speaker: Corporate Buyer
d4e3_corporate_b.text: We need 500 pounds of laundry processed by Friday at a 30% discount. This could be a recurring contract if you deliver. Can you make it happen?

choices.text: Accept the deal — big contract is big revenue
Negotiate the discount down to 15%
Accept the discount but push delivery to next week
Decline — cannot compromise quality for speed

d4e3_accept.speaker: Corporate Buyer
d4e3_accept.text: Excellent! We'll send the contract over. Make sure you deliver on time, or the deal is off.

d4e3_negotiate.speaker: Corporate Buyer
d4e3_negotiate.text: Fifteen percent? You drive a hard bargain. Let's meet at 20% and call it a deal.

d4e3_delay.speaker: Corporate Buyer
d4e3_delay.text: Next week works, actually. Quality matters more than speed to us. We appreciate the honesty.

d4e3_decline.speaker: Corporate Buyer
d4e3_decline.text: I understand. Perhaps we can revisit when your capacity grows.

d4e3_result_accept.speaker: Mentor
d4e3_result_accept.text: Big orders under pressure test your operations. Overcommitting can harm quality and burn out your team, even if the revenue looks good.

d4e3_result_negotiate.speaker: Mentor
d4e3_result_negotiate.text: Never accept the first offer in a negotiation! Pushing back respectfully often leads to better terms for both sides.

d4e3_result_delay.speaker: Mentor
d4e3_result_delay.text: Honest communication about your capacity builds long-term trust. A realistic promise kept beats an ambitious one broken.

d4e3_result_decline.speaker: Mentor
d4e3_result_decline.text: Knowing your limits is strength, not weakness. Protecting quality and team wellbeing keeps your business sustainable.

d4r_equipment_break.speaker: Staff
d4r_equipment_break.text: Boss! Machine #2 just stopped working mid-cycle. A customer's clothes are trapped inside, and there's water leaking.

d4r_equipment_b.speaker: You
d4r_equipment_b.text: A machine breakdown during a busy day. How do you handle the frustrated customer?

choices.text: Apologize, offer a free wash, and call repair immediately
Fix it yourself to save money
Redirect customer to another machine and deal later

d4r_equipment_apologize.speaker: Customer
d4r_equipment_apologize.text: Thank you for being so helpful. Accidents happen, and you handled it really well.

d4r_equipment_self.speaker: Staff
d4r_equipment_self.text: You fixed it, but it took two hours and stressed out the whole team.

d4r_equipment_redirect.speaker: Customer
d4r_equipment_redirect.text: So you're just going to ignore the broken machine? Not a good look.

d4r_equipment_result_apologize.speaker: Mentor
d4r_equipment_result_apologize.text: Good service recovery turns problems into loyalty opportunities. The cost was worth the trust you built.

d4r_equipment_result_self.speaker: Mentor
d4r_equipment_result_self.text: DIY repairs save money but cost time and team energy. Know when to call a professional.

d4r_equipment_result_redirect.speaker: Mentor
d4r_equipment_result_redirect.text: Avoiding problems doesn't make them disappear. Customers expect you to fix issues, not hide them.

d4r_supplier_delay.speaker: Mentor
d4r_supplier_delay.text: Your detergent supplier just called. Their shipment is delayed by three days due to a logistics issue.

d4r_supplier_b.speaker: You
d4r_supplier_b.text: Running low on detergent during a busy week is a real problem. What do you do?

choices.text: Buy from a more expensive local supplier temporarily
Ration existing supplies and hope for the best
Ask customers to bring their own detergent

d4r_supplier_local.speaker: Customer
d4r_supplier_local.text: Your detergent smells great today! Whatever you changed, keep it up.

d4r_supplier_ration.speaker: Customer
d4r_supplier_ration.text: The machines seem to be using less detergent. My clothes don't feel as clean.

d4r_supplier_bring.speaker: Customer
d4r_supplier_bring.text: I have to bring my own detergent? That's inconvenient. I might go elsewhere.

d4r_supplier_result_local.speaker: Mentor
d4r_supplier_result_local.text: Paying a premium to keep operations running is sometimes necessary. Customer trust is worth the temporary cost.

d4r_supplier_result_ration.speaker: Mentor
d4r_supplier_result_ration.text: Cutting corners on quality is visible to customers. Short-term savings can lead to long-term reputation damage.

d4r_supplier_result_bring.speaker: Mentor
d4r_supplier_result_bring.text: Shifting problems to customers damages loyalty. They come to you for convenience — make sure you provide it.

d4r_customer_compliment.speaker: Regular Customer
d4r_customer_compliment.text: I just wanted to say — this is the cleanest laundromat in the neighborhood. You're doing amazing work.

d4r_compliment_b.speaker: You
d4r_compliment_b.text: A genuine compliment from a regular customer. How do you respond?

choices.text: Thank them warmly and share the credit with your team
Thank them graciously and move on
Downplay it and get back to work

d4r_compliment_share.speaker: Staff
d4r_compliment_share.text: You told the customer it was a team effort? That means so much to all of us.

d4r_compliment_thank.speaker: Customer
d4r_compliment_thank.text: You're welcome! Just wanted you to know your hard work is noticed.

d4r_compliment_downplay.speaker: Customer
d4r_compliment_downplay.text: Oh, okay. Just thought you should know.

d4r_compliment_result_share.speaker: Mentor
d4r_compliment_result_share.text: Sharing credit builds team loyalty. A leader who lifts others up gets lifted higher in return.

d4r_compliment_result_thank.speaker: Mentor
d4r_compliment_result_thank.text: Gratitude is always welcome, but sharing the love costs nothing and builds culture.

d4r_compliment_result_downplay.speaker: Mentor
d4r_compliment_result_downplay.text: Humility is fine, but accepting praise graciously is also a skill. People want to feel heard.

d4e5_day_review.speaker: Mentor
d4e5_day_review.text: End of Day 4. You've faced challenges and made real progress. How do you want to approach Day 5?

d4e5_review_b.speaker: You
d4e5_review_b.text: The final day is tomorrow. What's your focus for the home stretch?

choices.text: Go all out — pull out all the stops
Stay steady — do what's been working
Conserve energy and resources

d4e5_allout.speaker: Staff
d4e5_allout.text: All out tomorrow? We're ready! Let's finish this week strong!

d4e5_steady.speaker: Staff
d4e5_steady.text: Steady and reliable. We can do that. Let's finish strong.

d4e5_conserve.speaker: Staff
d4e5_conserve.text: Taking it easy tomorrow? We'll do our best with what we have.

d4e5_result_allout.speaker: Mentor
d4e5_result_allout.text: Finishing strong matters. Tomorrow is your final chance to impact your grade. Make it count.

d4e5_result_steady.speaker: Mentor
d4e5_result_steady.text: Consistency wins many races. What you've built over four days is solid — tomorrow seals it.

d4e5_result_conserve.speaker: Mentor
d4e5_result_conserve.text: Preserving energy is valid, but don't coast on the final day. Every decision still matters.
